hypothesis: Concordance penalty on bayes_meta.py posteriors will fix inflated posteriors for contested frontiers (F-NK5: 0.988 with 3:3 C:F split)
method: Add discord-proportional pull toward 0.5 after sequential Bayesian update. discord = 2*min(C,F)/(C+F), pull = discord * 0.6. Applied before replication gate and conclusion assignment.
expect: F-NK5 posterior drops from 0.988 to 0.55-0.75. ECE in 0.6-0.8 bin drops from 0.194. Overall ECE stays below 0.15.
actual: F-NK5: 0.988→0.695 (MODERATE CONFIRM). 13 frontiers concordance-adjusted. 0.8-1.0 bin error 0.045→0.016 (2.8x improvement). 0.6-0.8 bin error 0.194→0.200 (slight increase from items moving in). Overall ECE 0.089→0.102 (still below 0.15).
diff: Expected F-NK5 in 0.55-0.75: CONFIRMED (0.695). Expected 0.6-0.8 bin improvement: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED — bin got slightly worse because contested items moved into it from 0.8-1.0. Pre-existing overconfidence in 0.6-0.8 range is independent of concordance fix. Novel finding: concordance fix improves high-confidence bin accuracy at cost of mid-confidence bin loading.
verdict: PARTIAL — concordance penalty fixes contested posteriors correctly but reveals deeper calibration issue in 0.6-0.8 range
design_notes: pull_factor=0.6: at max discord, posterior moves 60% toward 0.5
Applied BEFORE replication gate so both corrections compose
Does not affect frontiers with zero falsifications (majority)
